ac:rich-text-body.p: SKA Regional Centres (SRCs) are national or regional computing facilities that provide the global SKA user community with data access, storage, processing power, and expert support. Together, they form the SRCnet network — a distributed infrastructure enabling astronomers to conduct science with SKA data without needing to relocate to central observatories.

p: SKA Regional Centres are geographically distributed computing and data facilities operated by member countries and regional organisations. They provide:

ul.li: Data Access: Rapid access to SKA raw data, calibrated products, and archival holdings
Computing Resources: CPU and GPU clusters for data processing, analysis, and simulation
Storage Infrastructure: Petabyte-scale data repositories with redundancy and backup
User Support: Technical assistance, software support, and best-practice guidance
Training: Workshops, tutorials, and documentation for SKA data analysis

p: By distributing compute and storage across multiple centres, the SRCnet model implements the computation-to-data principle: bringing processing to where the data lives, minimising network traffic and reducing latency.
SRCnet is a federated network of interconnected regional centres, coordinated by the SKA Observatory. Centres share:

ul.li: Standardised data formats and metadata schemas
Cross-centre query and data discovery capabilities

p: This interconnection allows users to transparently access data and compute resources across multiple centres, choosing the facility best suited to their analysis needs.
The table below lists all SKA Regional Centres, their operating countries, and current status:

td.a: Max Planck Institute for Radio Astronomy

tr.td: Korea Astronomy and Space Science Institute

ac:rich-text-body.p: All SKA data becomes available through your regional centre shortly after observation and initial quality control. Data is provided in standardised formats (FITS, HDF5) with complete metadata and calibration tables. Bulk data downloads and streaming access are supported.

Regional centres provide containerised processing pipelines, Jupyter notebook environments, and access to standard analysis software (CASA, ASKAPsoft, etc.). GPU acceleration is available at many centres for image reconstruction and machine learning workloads.

Each centre maintains a helpdesk and technical support team. Support includes data discovery assistance, troubleshooting pipeline failures, optimising compute jobs, and answering SKA-specific questions. Response times typically range from hours to one business day.

Most regional centres host regular training sessions on data analysis, pipeline use, and advanced techniques. Many also participate in coordinated international training events run by the Observatory.

h2: How to Connect to Your Regional Centre

ac:rich-text-body.p: Select the centre serving your country or region from the table above. If your country is not yet listed, contact the SKA Observatory to learn about interim arrangements or planned centre development.

ol.li: Register: Create an account at your regional centre's portal (links provided in the table above).
Request Data Access: Propose your project in the Observatory's proposal system. Once approved, your centre will grant you access to relevant datasets.
Configure Your Environment: Your centre's support team will help you set up compute jobs, notebooks, and software environments tailored to your analysis.
Get Support: Contact your centre's helpdesk for technical issues, data questions, or analysis guidance.

p: Rather than downloading terabytes of raw data to your home institution, the SRCnet model brings your analysis code to the data. This approach offers several advantages:

ul.li: Speed: Avoid months of network transfers; process data at source.
Cost: Reduce bandwidth charges and local infrastructure investment.
Collaboration: Share results and intermediate products more easily with collaborators worldwide.
Sustainability: Lower carbon footprint by minimising data replication and long-distance transfers.

ac:rich-text-body.p: See the Science Operations documentation for detailed technical architecture, API documentation, and best-practice guides for working with your regional centre.
